description_long: This is a military-style utility helicopter with a streamlined, elongated fuselage painted in olive green with red accents. The helicopter features a prominent cockpit with large, rectangular windows and a slightly pointed nose. Its main rotor is mounted on top, and a tail boom extends rearward, ending in a vertical stabilizer and a small tail rotor. The landing gear consists of two parallel skids, providing stable ground support. The body appears to be constructed primarily from metal, with glass for the windows and reinforced composite materials for the rotors. The overall design is functional and robust, suitable for urban or tactical operations.
description: A green military-style utility helicopter with large cockpit windows, a top-mounted main rotor, and rear tail rotor, supported by parallel landing skids.
description_view_0: Side view showing the elongated fuselage, cockpit windows, main and tail rotors, and full length of the landing skids.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the wide cockpit windows, nose, and the parallel landing skids beneath the fuselage.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, showing the other profile of the fuselage, cockpit, and tail boom.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the tail boom, tail rotor, and the symmetrical arrangement of the landing skids.
attribute_rotor_blades: Four main rotor blades, long and narrow, likely made of composite material, colored dark gray or black with no visible markings.
attribute_tail_rotor: Two-blade tail rotor mounted vertically at the end of the tail boom, colored dark gray or black, no visible protective shroud.
attribute_landing_skids: Two parallel tubular landing skids, metallic with a dark finish, connected by crossbars for stability.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Four large, rectangular cockpit windows with rounded edges, clear with dark framing, arranged in a wraparound configuration at the front.
